summary: The implementation successfully covers the core wand collection and friend system requirements. All backend types and functions are implemented in backend/main.mo. Frontend pages, components, hooks, and routing are created as specified. The magical wand-themed visual style with wood tones, starlight accents, and serif typography is consistently applied. However, some acceptance criteria are not fully implemented or verifiable from the truncated diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Page shows list of confirmed friends with their names and profile pictures
missing_requirements.reason: The FriendsPage implementation is truncated in the diff. While the component structure suggests it displays friends with UserAvatar and profile names, the full implementation of the confirmed friends list rendering is not visible in the provided diff to confirm all AC details.

missing_requirements.requirement: Includes wand count for the friend
missing_requirements.reason: The backend getFriendWands function implementation is truncated. While the frontend FriendWandsPage displays wand count using wands.length, it is unclear if the backend function explicitly returns a count field as specified in the AC.

hallucinated_features.description: Magic type and system with wandId reference
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend defines a Magic type with fields id, name, effect, owner, wandId, and createdAt. This magic system with wand associations was not requested in any requirement and appears to be an extra feature beyond the spec.

REQ-30 specifies edit buttons on wands but does not explicitly require a separate EditWandForm component. While this is a reasonable implementation choice, it goes beyond the minimal requirements which could have used inline editing or a modal without a dedicated component file.

hallucinated_features.description: Marketplace-related types and data structures retained
hallucinated_features.reason: The backend retains Item, CartItem, ShoppingCart, Message types and associated data structures (items, shoppingCarts, messages maps). The build request explicitly lists marketplace functionality as a non-goal, though the constraint allows not modifying certain paths. These types appear unused for the wand collection app.
